title: Ixora tenuiflora

intro: Ixora tenuiflora, also known as jungle geranium, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ixora tenuiflora include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ixora tenuiflora is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and sore throats.
